name: Button
description: Buttons provide clear, high-confidence actions across the dashboard, from opening reports to confirming billing, while keeping the overall tone warm and editorial rather than overly technical.
summary: Use the button component for moments that need commitment, progression, or lightweight utility. The shape is generous and premium, the contrast is deliberate, and the copy should stay brief and decisive.

variants.name: primary
variants.useCase: Main call to action such as Save changes, Publish report, or Export summary.

variants.name: secondary
variants.useCase: Alternative actions that still deserve visibility, such as View details or Duplicate.

variants.name: ghost
variants.useCase: Low-emphasis inline actions inside cards, tables, and chart headers.

variants.name: destructive
variants.useCase: Irreversible decisions such as Remove member or Delete workspace.

sizes.name: compact
sizes.useCase: Toolbar actions and dense table contexts.

contentGuidelines: Start labels with a verb: Review invoice, Export CSV, Invite editor.
Keep labels under three words when possible.
Reserve destructive wording for truly irreversible actions.

accessibility.focusBehavior: Always expose a visible focus ring using the ring token.
accessibility.disabledBehavior: Disabled buttons remain readable but should not rely on color alone to communicate inactivity.
accessibility.iconRule: If an icon-only button is used, provide an accessible name with aria-label.

compositionNotes: Primary and destructive variants work best on neutral dashboard surfaces such as cards and page backgrounds.
Ghost buttons should appear on muted or card surfaces where the accent token can provide gentle affordance without overpowering nearby data.
Fraunces gives the control an editorial signature, so spacing must remain generous enough to preserve legibility.
